title: Understanding $$30-60-90$$ Triangles
text: In a $$30-60-90$$ triangle, the side opposite the 60-degree angle equals $$\frac{hypotenuse}{2} \sqrt{3}$$

title: Using the Hypotenuse
text: The hypotenuse is the longest side of a right triangle. In a $$30-60-90$$ triangle, first find the short leg by taking half the hypotenuse. Then multiply by $$\sqrt{3}$$ to get the side opposite 60°

title: Calculate the Opposite Side
text: Given that the hypotenuse is $$10$$ units, calculate the length of the side opposite the 30-degree angle using the ratio.
